chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُلْقِي الدَّوَابَّ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 96 – The one in Ihraam casts off the insects from himself

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/324)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/324)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ وَجَدْتُ عَلَيَّ قُرَاداً أَوْ حَلَمَةً أَطْرَحُهُمَا قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ صَغَارٌ لَهُمَا إِنَّهُمَا رَقِيَا فِي غَيْرِ مَرْقَاهُمَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Najran, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is your<sup>asws</sup> view if I were to find mite upon me, or a bug, can I throw these off?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and their young ones. They tend to ascend in other than their place of ascending’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Najran from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram finds ticks and bigger ticks; can he throw it away? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so. It is very mean of them to climb where they are not to climb.’”
